criticalRules: For single-input negation, set exprs="[-1]", realParameters height="1" width="1" with value -1.0, and keep exactly one ExplicitInputPort
Reference 2-input geometry uses height 60; keep the port count, sign vector, and geometry aligned with the exact variant you are building
Use scalar dataLines/dataColumns when the connected signals are scalar; reserve dataLines=-1 for genuinely variable-size signals
description: Big Summer block - sums multiple inputs with configurable signs
anomalies: exprs encodes input signs instead of using a separate operator parameter
Height 60 (1.5x standard) to accommodate multiple inputs
Single-input negation reuses the same block family with a 1x1 sign vector
name: BIGSOM_f
commonUses: Single-input negation in ODE feedback loops
